eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
elk: noun. Any of various large species of deer such as the red deer, moose or wapiti (see usage notes).
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
keel: noun. A large beam along the underside of a ship’s hull from bow to stern.
keelson: noun. A longitudinal beam fastened on top of the keel of a vessel for strength and stiffness.
keen: adjective. Often with a prepositional phrase, or with to and an infinitive: showing a quick and ardent responsiveness or willingness;
ken: verb. To give birth, conceive, beget, be born; to develop (as a fetus); to nourish, sustain (as life).
keno: noun. A gambling game, a variety of lotto, played with balls or knobs, numbered, and cards also numbered.
kent: verb. simple past and past participle of ken
keto: noun. The carbonyl group of a ketone.
ketone: noun. A homologous series of organic molecules whose functional group is an oxygen atom joined to a carbon atom, by a double bond, in a
ketose: noun. A saccharide containing a ketone functional group.
knee: noun. In humans, the joint or the region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank.
kneel: verb. To rest on one's bent knees, sometimes only one; to move to such a position.
kneels: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of kneel
knelt: verb. simple past and past participle of kneel.
knot: noun. A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of
koel: noun. A cuckoo of the genus Eudynamys, native to Asia, Australia and the Pacific.
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
leek: noun. A vegetable of variety Allium ampeloprasum, having edible leaves and an onion -like bulb but with a milder flavour than the onion.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
leet: noun. A portion or list, especially a list of candidates for an office; also the candidates themselves.
lek: noun. An aggregation of male animals for the purposes of courtship and display.
leno: noun. A type of weave used to make light, open cotton fabric used for window curtains.
lens: noun. An object, usually made of glass, that focuses or defocuses the light that passes through it.
lent: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lent.
leone: noun. A unit of currency of Sierra Leone, divided into 100 cents.
lest: conjunction. For fear that; that not; in order to prevent something from happening; in case.
let: verb. To allow to, not to prevent.
lose: verb. To cease to have (something) in one's possession or capability.
lost: verb. simple past and past participle of lose
lot: noun. A large quantity or number; a great deal.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
nestle: verb. To settle oneself comfortably and snugly.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
noel: noun. A kind of hymn, or canticle, of mediaeval origin, sung in honor of the birth of Christ; a Christmas carol.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
ole: interjection. An interjection used to stir up excitement.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
onset: noun. The initial phase of a disease or condition, in which symptoms first become apparent.
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seek: verb. To try to find; to look for; to search for.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
sente: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Lesotho loti.
set: verb. To put (something) down, to rest.

skeet: noun. A form of trapshooting using clay targets to simulate birds in flight.
skeleton: noun. The system that provides support to an organism, internal and made up of bones and cartilage in vertebrates, external in some other
skene: noun. An element of ancient Greek theater: the structure at the back of the stage.
sleek: adjective. Having an even, smooth surface; smooth
sleet: noun. Pellets of ice made of mostly- frozen raindrops or refrozen melted snowflakes.
sloe: noun. The small, astringent, wild fruit of the blackthorn (Prunus spinosa).
slot: noun. A narrow depression, perforation, or aperture; especially, one for the reception of a piece fitting or sliding into it.
snot: noun. Mucus, especially mucus from the nose.

sol: noun. In a movable - do or tonic sol-fa system: the fifth step in a scale, preceded by fa and followed by la.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
steek: verb. To stitch (sew with a needle).
steel: noun. An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.
stele: noun. An upright (or formerly upright) slab containing engraved or painted decorations or inscriptions; a stela.
steno: noun. a stenographer, someone whose job is to take dictation in shorthand
stone: noun. A hard earthen substance that can form rocks; especially, such substance when regarded as a building material.
tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).

tele: noun. Alternative spelling of telly (“television, telegraph, telephone, teleport, telecommunication”).

telson: noun. The part of an arthropod or crustacean posterior to the last segment, often resembling an armored tail or tip of the tail.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tense: noun. The property of indicating the point in time at which an action or state of being occurs or exists.
toe: noun. Each of the five digits on the end of the human foot.
token: noun. Something serving as an expression of something else.
tole: noun. A decorative metalware having a lacquered or enamelled surface that is painted or gilded.

tsk: interjection. Used to indicate disapproval, disappointment, or discontent.
eek: interjection. Representing a scream or shriek (especially in comic strips and books).
eeks: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of eek
elne: verb. To strengthen; hearten; comfort; encourage.
elt: verb. To injure (anything) by rough handling; handle roughly.

esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
kelt: noun. A thin, recently spawned iteroparous salmon.

kon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kongo.

oks: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oko-Eni-Osayen.
